turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="billGrad1" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#98e082" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#61b84a" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="billGrad2" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#b7f1a6" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#7fd56d" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="coinGrad" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffd766" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#d99e00" />
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="6" stdDeviation="8" flood-color="#000" flood-opacity="0.25" />
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- Stack of bills (three overlapping rounded rectangles) -->
  <g filter="url(#soft)">
    <!-- bottom bill -->
    <rect x="66" y="170" width="380" height="220" rx="20" fill="url(#billGrad1)" stroke="#4d8f36" stroke-width="6" />
    <!-- middle bill (slightly offset up-left) -->
    <rect x="56" y="150" width="380" height="220" rx="20" fill="url(#billGrad2)" stroke="#4d8f36" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.95" />
    <!-- top bill (front) -->
    <rect x="46" y="130" width="380" height="220" rx="20" fill="#89d46a" stroke="#3f7c30" stroke-width="6" />

    <!-- decorative emblems on top bill -->
    <!-- central emblem -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="240" r="36" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="4" />
    <circle cx="256" cy="240" r="20" fill="#7ab24a" />
    <!-- corner motifs -->
    <circle cx="110" cy="190" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />
    <circle cx="402" cy="190" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />
    <circle cx="110" cy="310" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />
    <circle cx="402" cy="310" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />

    <!-- subtle horizontal pattern lines on bills -->
    <g stroke="#6aa755" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.35">
      <path d="M86 200 H426" />
      <path d="M86 280 H426" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Coin overlapping the right side -->
  <g>
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="78" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#b37a00" stroke-width="6" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="52" fill="#ffd779" opacity="0.25" />
    <!-- rim highlight -->
    <path d="M300 300 C320 260, 400 260, 420 300" fill="none" stroke="#fff8b8" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.25" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- small inner ring -->
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="28" fill="#efb200" opacity="0.9" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="14" fill="#ffd" opacity="0.7" />
  </g>

  <!-- small pile shadow under composition -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="420" rx="160" ry="28" fill="#000" opacity="0.12" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{"svg":"<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="billGrad1" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#98e082" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#61b84a" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="billGrad2" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#b7f1a6" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#7fd56d" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="coinGrad" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffd766" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#d99e00" />
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="6" stdDeviation="8" flood-color="#000" flood-opacity="0.25" />
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- Stack of bills (three overlapping rounded rectangles) -->
  <g filter="url(#soft)">
    <!-- bottom bill -->
    <rect x="66" y="170" width="380" height="220" rx="20" fill="url(#billGrad1)" stroke="#4d8f36" stroke-width="6" />
    <!-- middle bill (slightly offset up-left) -->
    <rect x="56" y="150" width="380" height="220" rx="20" fill="url(#billGrad2)" stroke="#4d8f36" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.95" />
    <!-- top bill (front) -->
    <rect x="46" y="130" width="380" height="220" rx="20" fill="#89d46a" stroke="#3f7c30" stroke-width="6" />

    <!-- decorative emblems on top bill -->
    <!-- central emblem -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="240" r="36" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="4" />
    <circle cx="256" cy="240" r="20" fill="#7ab24a" />
    <!-- corner motifs -->
    <circle cx="110" cy="190" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />
    <circle cx="402" cy="190" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />
    <circle cx="110" cy="310" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />
    <circle cx="402" cy="310" r="14" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9" stroke="#cfead0" stroke-width="3" />

    <!-- subtle horizontal pattern lines on bills -->
    <g stroke="#6aa755" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.35">
      <path d="M86 200 H426" />
      <path d="M86 280 H426" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Coin overlapping the right side -->
  <g>
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="78" fill="url(#coinGrad)" stroke="#b37a00" stroke-width="6" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="52" fill="#ffd779" opacity="0.25" />
    <!-- rim highlight -->
    <path d="M300 300 C320 260, 400 260, 420 300" fill="none" stroke="#fff8b8" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.25" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- small inner ring -->
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="28" fill="#efb200" opacity="0.9" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="340" r="14" fill="#ffd" opacity="0.7" />
  </g>

  <!-- small pile shadow under composition -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="420" rx="160" ry="28" fill="#000" opacity="0.12" />
</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG for Money Representation**

I need to produce JSON that includes an SVG string to represent “money” using shapes only, without any text or numbers. I'm thinking about symbols like a stack of bills, coins, and maybe a dollar sign made from paths. I should avoid using any literal text. It might be more effective to just depict bills and coins using basic shapes: circular coins and rectangular banknotes without explicit dollar signs, perhaps including motifs like stripes and rounded corners. A simple piggy bank might also work!**Designing SVG for Currency Representation**

I’m going to create an SVG that’s 512x512. The design will feature a stack of three green bills, slightly offset, using rounded rectangles with a central circle emblem and two small corner circles to indicate currency. I’ll include a gold coin overlapping the bills. The fills and strokes will be simple, and no text will be included. I need to ensure it's valid JSON by escaping any quotes within the SVG string. The bills will vary in green shades, with strokes and a centered composition. Let's get this crafted!**Creating Shape Coordinates for SVG**

I'm designing the shapes for the SVG with a center at coordinates (256, 240). The bills will be 380 in width and 220 in height, which feels better than previous estimates. For the stacking: I'll place the bottom bill at y=170, the middle at y=150, and the top at y=130 to ensure they overlap correctly. The coin will sit in the lower right, around (360, 310) with a radius of 70. I'll add a central emblem circle and corner circles, making sure there's no text, and start building the SVG string.**Preparing SVG Composition**

I'm getting ready to compose the SVG. The XML declaration is optional, but I think it’s safer to skip it since the rule specifies a complete `<svg>...</svg>` string. I can include definitions for gradients and shadows using filters, but I'll keep it simple. As I craft the string, I need to remember to escape double quotes for the JSON by replacing them with `\"`. Now, let's prepare the JSON to make everything work smoothly!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
